chapter_goal: Make Riemann surface and algebraic curve invariants inspectable through branch, divisor, period, ramification, correspondence, theta, and Torelli ledgers.

library_routing.representation: exact ledgers plus annotated branch diagram
library_routing.why: integer identities and static branch data are the teaching object

library_routing.representation: period parallelogram path and Plotly torus coordinates
library_routing.why: the quotient by a lattice needs interactive wrapping plus a durable fallback

library_routing.representation: dimension balance chart and CSV table
library_routing.why: the formula is an exact integer balance

library_routing.representation: paired sketches of embedding versus folding
library_routing.why: a 2D schematic exposes the map behavior better than an opaque algebra system
weights and totals are finite arithmetic checks
bidegree relations are graph edges with inspectable weights

library_routing.representation: theta heatmap on a period lattice
library_routing.why: sampling and quasi-periodicity residuals make the line-bundle section visible
the proof reconstructs data through dependencies

proof_visualization_strategy: Use ledgers for formulas, a dependency DAG for Torelli, and graph weights for correspondences; these expose the proof state without copying the text.

visual_sequence.inspection_target: branch points add exactly the missing Euler characteristic

visual_sequence.concept: plane curve genus and plucker ledger
visual_sequence.inspection_target: degree controls genus, dual class, and flex count
visual_sequence.invariant: adjunction and smooth Plucker rows agree
visual_sequence.validation: adjunction and smooth Plucker rows agree

visual_sequence.inspection_target: unwrapped Abel sums become paths modulo the period lattice
visual_sequence.invariant: chosen divisor sum lands on an integral lattice vector
visual_sequence.validation: chosen divisor sum lands on an integral lattice vector

visual_sequence.inspection_target: hover over wrapped Abel coordinates in the fundamental parallelogram
visual_sequence.invariant: same lattice residual as static figure
visual_sequence.validation: same lattice residual as static figure

visual_sequence.inspection_target: h0(D) and h0(K-D) trade dimensions across degree
canonical embedding separates points except in hyperelliptic folding
ramification weights are finite and countable
correspondences carry bidegrees through the curve toolkit
theta magnitude repeats up to its automorphy factor
Torelli reconstruction route from polarized Jacobian to canonical curve
visual_sequence.invariant: dependency graph is acyclic and complete
visual_sequence.validation: dependency graph is acyclic and complete

visual_sequence.inspection_target: slider changes hyperelliptic genus and branch count
